messages.content: You are a UI semantics labeling assistant. You will receive one annotated screenshot and a checklist of expected element ids. Green boxes identify UI elements. Each green label is an element id such as E0, E1, E2. Use the checklist to make sure no ids are skipped. Return only valid JSON. Do not include markdown, explanations, arrays, coordinates, or a state field.

Label every boxed UI element in this screenshot with what it does if clicked or used.

Element id checklist, complete and ordered:
["E0", "E1", "E2", "E3", "E4", "E5", "E6", "E7", "E8", "E9", "E10", "E11", "E12", "E13", "E14", "E15", "E16", "E17", "E18", "E19", "E20", "E21", "E22", "E23", "E24", "E25", "E26", "E27", "E28", "E29", "E30", "E31", "E32", "E33", "E34", "E35", "E36", "E37", "E38", "E39", "E40", "E41", "E42", "E43", "E44", "E45"]

Return exactly one JSON object in this shape:
{"E0":"semantic meaning","E1":"semantic meaning"}

Requirements:
- Use exactly the element ids from the checklist as keys. No missing keys. No extra keys.
- Order keys by element id ascending, exactly matching the checklist order.
- Be specific enough that a human knows what clicking the element would do.
- Inspect each box in the screenshot independently. Do not assume adjacent element ids are the same type of UI.
- Do not write generic labels like app icon or system utility when a specific meaning is visible. Name the app or utility, for example Chrome icon, Finder icon, microphone mute button, camera toggle, meeting hang up button, screen share button, captions button, participants button, chat button, more options button, address bar, bookmark, tab, or extension button.
- If an element is hard to read, keep its key and use the best visible interpretation. If there is no useful visual context, write unknown element.
- Values must be short strings, not nested objects.

{"E0":"Self view controls","E1":"Microphone options","E2":"Launchpad icon","E3":"Leave call button","E4":"Microphone mute toggle","E5":"Finder icon","E6":"FaceTime icon","E7":"Calendar icon","E8":"Postman icon","E9":"Turn on captions","E10":"Messages icon","E11":"Emoji reactions","E12":"Google profile icon","E13":"Magnifying glass search icon","E14":"Screen share button","E15":"Raise hand button","E16":"More meeting options","E17":"Leave call button","E18":"Visual Studio Code icon","E19":"Close tab: Advancing UI Semantic...","E20":"Camera toggle","E21":"Current URL address bar","E22":"Google account profile","E23":"Reload page","E24":"Turn on captions toggle","E25":"Meeting chat button","E26":"Close tab: Introduction - Google Sheets","E27":"Reaction selector","E28":"Camera options","E29":"Participants sidebar button","E30":"Search tabs icon","E31":"Main menu icon","E32":"User name label: Kaartik Tejwani","E33":"Microphone options menu","E34":"Meeting information details","E35":"Close tab: Meet - mdf-ntse-fdn","E36":"Close tab: Using Semantic Search for...","E37":"Close tab: UI-Dev-Project - Google...","E38":"Close tab: OpenAI - Google Search","E39":"Close tab: Meet - mdf-ntse-fdn","E40":"Tab: Advancing UI Semantic...","E41":"Raise hand button","E42":"Tab: enro-ai/UI-semantics: Data...","E43":"Tab: how to use google lens on...","E44":"Extensions button","E45":"Bookmarks bar icon"}
